title: uniface.DSPInstance
text: uniface.DSPInstance The uniface.DSPInstance object provides access to selected properties of the DSP component instance that it represents. Deprecated. Important: The object uniface.DSPInstance is deprecated. Use uniface.Instance instead. For more information, see uniface.Instance . Functions Table 1. Data Addressing Functions Function Description getName() Returns the name of the uniface.Instance . getShortName() Returns the short name of the current uniface.Instance . For a DSPInstance , this is the same as its name. However, other API objects have a similar function that returns shorter names than getName . getType() Returns a constant string denoting the uniface.Instance type: uniface.TYPE_DSPINSTANCE . getParent() Returns null because a uniface.Instance is always a top-level object. getComponentName() Returns the component name of this uniface.Instance . getEntities() Returns an array of uniface.Entity objects representing the outer entities of the uniface.Instance . getEntity() Returns the uniface.Entity object that represents the named outer entity. isEqualTo() Checks whether the uniface.Instance object is the same as another uniface.getInstance object. Table 2. Invocation API Functions Function Description activate() Activate an operation or supported trigger on the current DSP instance. The called operation can be executed on the client or the server, depending on how it is declared. remove Detach and delete the current DSP instance, detaching any child instances. Description The uniface.Instance object is returned by the uniface.getInstance function. The getParent function of a uniface.Entity object can also return a uniface.Instance object. Related Links getInstance() Returns a reference to the uniface.Instance object that represents a given DSP instance name on the browser. getInstance() Returns a reference to the uniface.Instance object that contains the current object. getParent() Returns an Uniface JavaScript object that represents the parent of the current object. DSP Component Instances DSP component instances differ from those of other components in that they are split—a non-persistent part runs on the server, and a persistent part runs on the browser. Communication between the two parts is asynchronous, via HTTP requests and responses over the internet. Uniface automatically handles communication and synchronization. Was this topic helpful?
